definition: Ability of the system or strategy to continue functioning under stress
measurement_note: Persistence is positive only when paired with harm reduction equity ecology and transformation

definition: Ability to reduce direct and indirect harm over time
measurement_note: Use exposure health ecological labor housing and recovery indicators

definition: Ability to reduce path dependence and preserve future options
measurement_note: Use infrastructure finance regulatory cultural and technological lock-in indicators

definition: Ability to reduce unequal vulnerability and avoid shifting risk to exposed groups
measurement_note: Use disaggregated exposure recovery access burden and participation indicators

definition: Ability to redesign harmful systems rather than restoring them
measurement_note: Use transition pathways alternatives governance authority finance and anti-lock-in indicators

definition: Ability to protect or restore ecological function biodiversity buffers water land and climate stability
measurement_note: Use ecosystem indicators habitat restoration emissions land-use and water metrics

definition: Degree to which continuity is achieved by shifting cost risk labor displacement pollution or ecological harm elsewhere
measurement_note: Higher values are penalized because they signal maladaptive resilience risk

definition: Administrative technical financial political and participation burden required to implement the strategy
measurement_note: Higher values require sequencing capacity and public finance review
